artifacts.content:
Subject: Catching you up on everything (it’s a lot)

Hi Rosa,

I’ve been meaning to sit down and write you a real email instead of those little texts. I’m on the couch with my feet up, BP cuff next to me, so it’s a good time.

My breathing’s been a little worse on the stairs again, and every time it happens I go right back to that night in March 2019 when I had the heart attack alone here at 1842 East Rockwell. I can still hear the MetroHealth paramedics breaking the chain on the door and me thinking I was going to die before Elena could get here from Lakewood. When the nurse asks about shortness of breath now, that whole movie plays in my head.

The anxiety hasn’t helped since July, when Mateo’s other grandma passed in her sleep in Parma. Now I wake up 5–6 times a night checking my pulse and picturing Carlos finding me like he found her. Some nights I even take an extra half lorazepam Dr. Patel gave me, even though he told me not to change the dose. I know, I know.

Money is still tight. My Social Security is only $1,486 a month, and after $780 rent here on Rockwell and about $220 for the heart pills and blood pressure meds, there’s not much left. I can’t keep paying $18 each way for Lyft to Dave’s Market on Ridge, which is how I ended up with almost no groceries for three days last February. I’m trying to see if there’s a food pantry closer by or more rides.

Anyway, Miguel is coming over later with pan dulce, and we’ll probably just watch our novelas. Call me tonight if you’re free; I miss hearing your voice.

Te quiero,
Patty

metadata.subject: Catching you up on everything (it’s a lot)

artifacts.content:
Subject: Why I was such a mess last fall

Miguel,

I’ve been thinking about how I acted those weeks after October and wanted you to understand the whole picture.

You remember when Gabi had that overdose scare at her high school in Lakewood in October 2024? Elena called me screaming, and we rushed from here to the MetroHealth ER on West 25th and stayed there all night. Even though they said it was “just” an attempt and she’d physically be okay, I can’t forget seeing her hooked up to monitors. Ever since then, whenever I have to leave the apartment or ride the van to appointments, I get this wave of panic like that night is happening again.

On top of that, July 2024 really shook me when Mateo’s other grandmother died in her sleep in Parma. Between that and my own heart stuff, every time I wake up at 2 or 3 in the morning to check my pulse, I picture Carlos finding me the way he found her. That’s when I grab my phone and start doom‑scrolling or I text you, even if I don’t hit send.

You know I asked them to be okay with you staying over more nights because sleeping alone brings back those old memories of Daniel showing up drunk at my West 25th place in the early 2000s, banging on the door at 2 or 3 a.m. I still jump at any noise in the hallway.

Thank you for not making me feel crazy about all this, and for bringing pan dulce and just sitting with me when my chest feels tight.

Con cariño,
Patricia

metadata.subject: Why I was such a mess last fall

artifacts.content:
Title: Money + meds reality check

Date: 2024-10-05

I need this all in one place so I stop pretending it’s not happening.

Income: Social Security check is $1,486/month.

Regular bills:
- Rent at 1842 E. Rockwell, Apt 3B: $780
- Heart/BP meds (approx): $220
- Phone/internet: around $90
That leaves very little for food, bus fare, copays.

Since the back surgery at Cleveland Clinic Lutheran Hospital in July 2024, the Medicaid spend‑down/share of cost is $420. After that, I sometimes end up with only about $60 a month for groceries. No wonder I can’t always afford the #51 bus or a Lyft to MetroHealth Old Brooklyn for PT, and I cancel.

Other money drains I don’t admit out loud:
- Helping Miguel $100/month since his pension got cut in January, which means I sometimes delay picking up my Eliquis at the CVS on West 25th for a week or two.
- That $47 sertraline copay I skipped for three weeks back in March so I could help Elena with her overdue gas bill.
- Lyft to Dave’s Market on Ridge Road is about $18 each way. If I go twice, that’s groceries. Last February I went three days with almost no real food because I was trying to “stretch it.”

Action ideas: Ask Ellen about emergency prescription help, food pantry options, and maybe a cheaper way to get to PT and the store so I don’t keep choosing between meds and groceries.

Subject: Hard things I didn’t say in front of the kids

Elena,

Thank you again for driving me to MetroHealth Old Brooklyn the other day. I didn’t want to upset you in the car with Gabi there, but there are a few things on my mind.

First, I’m still thinking about that Sunday Mass at St. Bartholomew on December 3, 2023, when Father Luis had to stop the service and call 911 because I thought I was having another heart attack. I can still feel everyone’s eyes on me as they wheeled me out on the stretcher. Talking about going back to church still makes my chest tight.

I also keep replaying that night at 1842 East Rockwell in late March 2024 when I sat here alone and actually lined up my heart pills and thought about taking the whole bottle. I scared myself. I think skipping the sertraline for those three weeks so I could help you with the gas bill didn’t help my thinking.

And I’m still ashamed about what happened at Carlos’s house in Old Brooklyn last November, when he grabbed my arm during that argument about me not signing over part of my Social Security check and left bruises. Ellen talked with me about a safety plan, but it breaks my heart that we even have to think about that with my own son.

On top of everything, worrying about Gabi’s panic attacks since your brother’s DUI last Thanksgiving and Mateo’s autism evaluation at Rainbow Babies on October 3, 2024 has me stretched thin. Some days I can barely remember my leg exercises.

I don’t tell you all this to make you feel guilty, mija. I just want you to know why I seem so jumpy and tired.

Te amo,
Mami

metadata.subject: Hard things I didn’t say in front of the kids

artifacts.content:
Title: Night thoughts – don’t forget to mention

Date: 2025-11-19

Couldn’t sleep again, so writing it down.

1. St. Vincent ER last week – Dr. Kaplan at St. Vincent Charity Medical Center ER said my congestive heart failure has “progressed” and if things don’t improve we might have to talk about hospice within 6–12 months. I heard “hospice” and stopped listening. Need to ask what I can still do to delay that – exercises, salt, meds, etc.

2. Still haunted by March 18, 2023, when MetroHealth Medical Center ER said I’d had a small heart attack a few days after Dr. Patel at MetroCardio on Franklin brushed off my chest pain as “just anxiety” from the divorce. That’s why I get so scared they’ll miss something.

3. Family stress is stealing my focus:
   - Gabi’s self‑harm scare in May 2025 and now the $275/month for her counseling at Metro Behavioral Health Clinic. Daniel and I are already two months behind.
   - October 2, 2025 guardianship hearing at Cuyahoga County Probate Court about Mateo. Judge brought up my hospitalizations at Cleveland Clinic Fairview as a reason I shouldn’t be backup guardian. Felt like my whole year of trying was invisible.

4. Meds I’m embarrassed about:
   - Nights I wake up 5–6 times checking my pulse and take an extra half lorazepam.
   - Times I’ve skipped the evening water pill if I’m riding the #51 bus and don’t have $12–15 for an Uber in case of an accident.

Talk about all this with Ellen and maybe the social worker before the next cardiology visit.

metadata.title: Night thoughts – don’t forget to mention

artifacts.content:
Subject: Stuff I never told you about that meeting

Maria,

I thought of you after my last appointment and figured you’d understand.

Do you remember when we met for coffee after my heart cath last year and I told you I felt “watched”? Part of it goes back to January 8, 2025 at the St. Vincent Charity outpatient building. The nurse practitioner, Ms. Reynolds, brought up, in front of everyone, how I told Dr. Shah at MetroHealth Behavioral back in March 2023 that I’d thought about taking all my clonazepam at once, and how sometimes I hear my mom’s voice at night. I know they’re trying to help, but I felt so exposed, like they were going to stamp “crazy” on my forehead.

I already carry shame from July 2019 when the urgent care at Parma Health Center put that “early refill behavior” note in my chart about the pain pills. That’s part of why, when my knees scream now, I sometimes sneak one of Miguel’s leftover hydrocodone from his 2022 knee surgery instead of asking my own doctor.

Add in all the family things — Mateo’s autism evaluation at Rainbow Babies & Children’s on October 3, 2024 where I sobbed in front of Dr. Singh, and Gabi’s overdose scare at her Lakewood high school — and I feel like my nerves are always on the outside of my skin.

Day‑to‑day, life is still the same: nurse on Tuesdays, PT twice a week, trying to keep up with the hallway walks and not eat too much salt. But inside, it’s noisy.

Thanks for always listening without judging.

Abrazos,
Patricia

metadata.subject: Stuff I never told you about that meeting

artifacts.content:
Title: Busy health + family week – don’t overdo it

Date range: 2024-04-15 to 2024-04-22

Mon 4/15
- Morning: BP, weight, leg wound check at home. Write numbers in notebook.
- Call CVS on West 25th about Eliquis refill – make sure I don’t delay it again like when I was giving Miguel that $100/month after his pension got cut.

Tue 4/16
- 10:00 a.m. – Visiting nurse (med review, leg dressing).
- Ask about that night in late March when I lined up my heart meds at 1842 E. Rockwell and scared myself thinking about taking them all.

Wed 4/17
- PT at MetroHealth Old Brooklyn (practice hallway walking with walker). Pack snack so I’m not tempted by vending machines.

Thu 4/18
- 2:00 p.m. – Cardiology check at MetroCardio Associates.
  Notes: Bring list of meds, mention skipping sertraline for three weeks in March because of Elena’s gas bill, and that scary night I thought about taking all my heart pills.

Sun 4/21
- Church at St. Bartholomew if I feel up to it. Still nervous after the Dec 3 panic attack when Father Luis had to stop Mass and call 911.

Mon 4/22
- 11:00 a.m. – Visit at MetroHealth Old Brooklyn Campus.
  Notes: Talk about Gabi’s panic attacks since Carlos’s DUI last Thanksgiving and how worrying about her makes it harder to focus on home exercises and BP checks.

Reminder to self: Schedule rest time after each outing so I don’t crash and forget evening meds.

metadata.title: Busy health + family week – don’t overdo it
